commonIssues:
Mistakes in spelling names of the deceased or family members in the digital system are common, requiring legal corrections. The Karnataka High Court has noted frequent such errors.

How to fix:

  • Visit the Nadakacheri (AJSK) Portal or your nearest Atalji Janasnehi Kendra.
  • Submit a written correction request with a Notarised Affidavit and supporting proof (hospital record, Aadhaar).
  • If the error is in the issued certificate, return the original and request a corrected copy from the ULB (Urban Local Body) office.
  • Helpline: 080-4455-4455 (Karnataka Nadakacheri)

Deaths occurring at home require more complex documentation from local authorities compared to hospital deaths.

How to fix:

  • Obtain a certificate from a Registered Medical Practitioner (RMP) or nearby Primary Health Centre (PHC).
  • Get a Panchanama from the local police station confirming the death.
  • Submit these along with Form No. 2 to the Gram Panchayat or ULB office within 21 days.
  • Reference: CRS Portal Guidelines

Difficulty downloading the generated certificate due to digital signature issues is commonly reported on Karnataka portals.

How to fix:

  • Try downloading using a different browser (Chrome recommended) or device.
  • Ensure your device has the latest Java Runtime Environment (JRE) for digital signature verification.
  • If download consistently fails, visit the ULB or Gram Panchayat office to request a printed, sealed copy.
  • Report issues to: Nadakacheri Portal or call 080-4455-4455.

Applications sometimes remain pending for extended periods with the Village Accountant or Revenue officials despite submission.

How to fix:

  • Track your application status on the Nadakacheri Portal using your acknowledgement number.
  • If pending beyond 15 working days, escalate to the Tehsildar's office with a written complaint.
  • File a grievance via the Karnataka Public Grievance Redressal System (PGRS).
  • Contact: Karnataka Janaspandana helpline 1902

Despite online systems, obtaining the final certificate is often delayed, forcing citizens to make manual visits to ULB offices.

How to fix:

  • Check the status at CRS Portal or Nadakacheri using your registration number.
  • If status shows "approved" but certificate not issued, visit the ULB office with a printout of the approval.
  • Raise a formal complaint on the Karnataka PGRS portal.
